ICAC1.principle: Cheating in the knowledge test is misconduct in an official licensing assessment.
ICAC1.application: The question asks what happens after being caught cheating. The penalty is a temporary ban from another attempt, so the applicant must wait 6 weeks before taking the test again.
ICAC1.watchOut: The penalty is not permanent, but it is also not ignored.

ICAC2.principle: Offering a bribe to pass a driving test is corruption in the licensing process.
ICAC2.application: Because the applicant is the person offering the bribe, action can be taken against that applicant, including serious penalties such as fines and imprisonment.
ICAC2.watchOut: It is not only the testing officer who can be investigated.

ICAC3.principle: A bribe is still corruption even if the testing officer asks for it first.
ICAC3.application: If the applicant pays the bribe, everyone involved has taken part in the corrupt act, so action can be taken against all involved.
ICAC3.watchOut: Being asked for a bribe does not make paying it lawful.
